title: 10 best herbal teas for acne

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander teas helps with acne because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as germanderin and limonene, work to reduce the production of sebum and kill off Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that contributes to acne formation. Additionally, germander teas' antiseptic qualities help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with acne breakouts. Regular consumption of germander tea may lead to clearer, healthier-looking skin over time.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Germander tea has antimicrobial properties that help combat acne-causing bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in germander tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, and also help soothe irritated skin.

Antioxidant properties: Germander tea is rich in antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and contribute to acne. Antioxidants also help promote healthy skin cell turnover, reducing the appearance of acne scars.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Barks: Contain antibacterial properties that aid in killing bacteria responsible for causing acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves according to local regulations and guidelines for sustainable foraging practices.
Measure one teaspoon of dried germander leaves or two teaspoons of fresh germander leaves per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the germander leaves in boiling water for ten to fifteen minutes to allow for optimal extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup and discard the solids after preparation is complete.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before consumption, as it can be taken undiluted or mixed with other herbal teas as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The flavonoids and terpenes present in the herb have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with acne, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and calming the skin. Additionally, the antiseptic qualities help to combat bacterial overgrowth, which is a common underlying cause of acne, reducing the severity and frequency of breakouts. As a result, pot marigold teas can be a valuable addition to any acne treatment routine.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can exacerbate acne.

Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial agents, including luteolin and apigenin, work to combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne, by inhibiting their growth and activity.
Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acne development and severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce acne inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain flavonoids and terpenes that exhibit antibacterial and antioxidant activities, helping to combat acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water needed.
Combine the measured dried flowers and boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to release into the liquid.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any loose flower particles and sediment.
Pour the tea into a cup and enjoy immediately, or store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with acne because of its potent antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and purify the blood. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also aid in soothing redness and swelling associated with pimples. Additionally, dandelion roots contain inulin, a prebiotic that promotes a healthy gut microbiome, which is linked to clear skin. By drinking dandelion tea regularly, individuals can help regulate their body's natural detoxification processes, leading to a reduction in acne breakouts and a clearer complexion overall.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Dandelion tea contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are major contributors to acne development.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion tea, such as kaempferol and quercetin, can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Detoxifying Properties: Dandelion tea is rich in prebiotic fiber that can help regulate gut bacteria, reducing toxins and waste products that can clog pores and cause acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and other nutrients, they help to reduce sebum production and unclog pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried dandelion leaves and flowers in amounts of 2 teaspoons for every 8 ounces water.
Combine the ingredients with boiling water in a teapot or infuser and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids after 5 minutes of preparation time.
Allow the tea to cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it for later use within 24 hours.
Drink the tea twice daily, warm or at room temperature, as needed for acne relief over a period of two weeks duration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation caused by acne, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and calming the skin. Additionally, chamomile's antibacterial agents help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne, allowing the tea to promote healthy skin and reduce the occurrence of breakouts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile tea contains apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, promoting a smoother skin complexion.

Antimicrobial activity: The flavonoids present in chamomile tea, such as apigenin and quercetin, exhibit antimicrobial properties that can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, which can contribute to the development of acne.

Calming and soothing effects: Chamomile tea's calming and soothing properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are known triggers for acne. By promoting relaxation, chamomile tea may also help regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which help soothe and calm skin irritations associated with acne.
Flowers: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, flowers are often used to reduce redness and swelling caused by acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chamomile flowers and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Combine one tablespoon of dried or two tablespoons of fresh chamomile flowers with 8 ounces boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the tea to infuse and release its active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Drink the tea immediately after preparation or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe teas helps with acne because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that work to soothe and calm the skin. The antioxidants present in aloe vera help reduce inflammation, which can lead to acne, while also fighting off bacteria that can cause breakouts. Additionally, aloe tea has been shown to balance the skin's natural pH levels, reducing the production of sebum and helping to unclog pores. By drinking this tea regularly, individuals can experience a reduction in acne symptoms and enjoy healthier, clearer-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne by decreasing inflammation in the skin.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial compounds present in aloe vera tea, including salicylic acid and lupeol, work to combat the bacterial growth that can cause acne by inhibiting the multiplication of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria.

Wound healing and antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's wound-healing compounds, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help promote skin repair and regeneration, reducing the appearance of acne scars and promoting a faster recovery from breakouts.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, aloe roots help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Leaves: Containing soothing gel and salicylic acid, aloe leaves calm inflammation and prevent scarring from acne.
remedy_recipe: Wash your hands thoroughly before handling aloe vera leaves and utensils.
Cut off the top third of an aloe vera leaf and remove the thick skin on both sides.
Scoop out the clear gel inside the leaf with a spoon and measure out 2 tablespoons.
Combine the aloe vera gel with boiling water in a cup, letting it steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the solids, then drink immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with acne because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically or consumed as a tea, glycyrrhizin can reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne. Additionally, the antioxidants present in licorice root have been shown to help combat free radicals that contribute to skin damage and premature aging. By reducing inflammation and promoting antioxidant activity, herbal licorice teas can help to improve overall skin health and reduce the appearance of acne lesions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, making it an effective natural remedy for calming skin irritations.

Antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial agents in licorice tea, including triterpenes and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that contributes to acne formation.

Hormone-balancing properties: Licorice root has been found to regulate hormone levels, particularly cortisol, which can contribute to acne development by stimulating oil production and inflammation in the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing acne symptoms such as redness and inflammation.
Barks: Licorice barks contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing acne breakouts and promoting healthy skin tone.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of dried licorice root and rinse it gently with cold running water.
Combine the licorice root with 8 ounces of boiling water in a medium-sized teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Consume the cooled tea immediately or store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ironwort teas helps with acne because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacteria that can cause inflammation and clogged pores. The tea's flavonoids and saponins also help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, leading to a decrease in acne severity. Additionally, ironwort's antioxidant activity helps to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy and clear skin. By drinking ironwort teas regularly, individuals can enjoy a natural and effective way to combat acne and achieve radiant, glowing skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ironwort tea is rich in antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by acne, thus preventing further damage to the skin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Ironwort tea can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, allowing for faster healing and reduced scarring.
Antibacterial properties: Ironwort tea's antibacterial properties help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), the bacteria that causes acne, by inhibiting its growth and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ironwort plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Seeds: Ironwort seeds contain antioxidants that help combat free radicals, which can contribute to skin damage and inflammation caused by acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sideritis scardica leaves and flowers, ensuring at least 30 grams of plant material for the tea batch.
Rinse the harvested sideritis scardica plant material gently with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried sideritis scardica leaves and flowers or three teaspoons of fresh plant material with one cup of boiling distilled water in a large teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the water and release its active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing the tea in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with acne because its active compounds, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, possess potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots and dry them properly for future use.
Measure one teaspoon of dried echinacea root for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the echinacea root in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids after the preparation time is complete.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consumption or refrigerate for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Agrimony teas helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh agrimony leaves and stems, rinse them thoroughly under running water.

Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes, allowing the herbal goodness to infuse.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Allow the tea to cool, then store it in the refrigerator for up to three days consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein teas helps with acne because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. The tea's antioxidants also help to combat free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to clogged pores. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Mullein tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne by reducing inflammation in the skin.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mullein leaves and flowers or purchase dried organic herb in bulk for a minimum of 100 grams.
Rinse the harvested mullein material with cold filtered water to remove any dirt or debris then chop it finely into small pieces.
Combine one teaspoon of chopped mullein herb with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass teapot or ceramic infuser.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes allowing the herbal compounds to infuse and release their therapeutic properties slowly.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug discarding any solids and enjoy warm within the next hour for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw teas helps with acne because it contains harpagoside, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This property can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne lesions, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing the skin. remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried harpagophytum procumbens root powder into a clean tea infuser or strainer.
Boil fresh water in a teapot and pour it over the herb, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup, discarding any solids remaining behind.
Add one tablespoon of raw honey to the tea and stir until dissolved, sweetening the brew to taste.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, sipping slowly throughout the day as needed.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried taraxacum kok saghyz leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to the tea, stirring until dissolved, and let it cool to room temperature.
Store the tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or drink immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Canada thistle teas helps with acne because its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried cirsium arvense flowers and 4 cups of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in hot water for 10-15 minutes, allowing them to infuse their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large pitcher, discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Pour 1-2 cups of cooled tea into a glass filled with ice and enjoy as a refreshing drink two times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright teas helps with acne because it contains antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and antibacterial properties that work synergistically to soothe and calm the skin. Bring filtered or spring water to a boil and then pour it over the euphrasia officinalis leaves, allowing them to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean teapot or mug, discarding the solids.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, the roots of the Chinese Thoroughwort plant help to reduce inflammation and calm the skin, making it an effective ingredient in teas for acne-prone skin.
Rhyzomes: As a rich source of flavonoids, rhyzomes in tea form can help to soothe and regulate hormonal imbalances that may contribute to acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bupleurum falcatum roots and clean them thoroughly with cold water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm oven at 150°f for 24 hours or air dry.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried bupleurum falcatum roots per cup of boiling water and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea leaves and discard the solids before serving and store leftover tea in an airtight container.
Drink 2-3 cups of the prepared tea daily to help alleviate acne symptoms within a week.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey teas helps with acne because of its antibacterial properties, which help to combat the underlying causes of acne. The tea's high concentration of allantoin and rosmarinic acid work together to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with acne. Additionally, comfrey's natural antiseptic qualities help to prevent bacterial growth and infection, promoting a healthy balance in the skin's ecosystem and ultimately clearing up acne-prone areas. Regular consumption of comfrey tea can lead to clearer, more radiant-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Comfrey tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, allowing the skin to heal more effectively.

Antibacterial properties: The tannins present in comfrey tea have antibacterial effects, which can help to combat P. acnes bacteria that cause acne by reducing their growth and preventing them from producing toxins that clog pores.

Wound-healing properties: Comfrey's mucilage content helps to create a protective barrier on the skin, promoting wound healing and reducing the appearance of scars, while also soothing and calming irritated skin prone to acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a powerful anti-inflammatory agent that helps soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Leaves: High in vitamin B12 and other essential nutrients that promote healthy skin cell growth and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried symphytum officinale leaves into a heat-resistant cup.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 212 degrees fahrenheit in a kettle.
Pour the heated water over the symphytum officinale leaves and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a clean cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a tea infuser.
Allow the tea to cool down to room temperature before drinking within 30 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for acne are a natural and effective way to combat skin breakouts, utilizing the healing properties of plants to soothe and calm the skin. These teas work by reducing inflammation, balancing hormones, and purifying the bloodstream, which can help to prevent acne from forming or reduce its severity. Examples of beneficial herbal teas include tea tree oil-infused tea, green tea, and chamomile tea, which have been shown to reduce acne symptoms such as redness, swelling, and scarring. By incorporating these teas into one's daily routine, individuals can enjoy improved skin clarity and a sense of confidence, leading to a more radiant and healthy appearance.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat acne with herbal teas is to consume a daily dose of green tea, which contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and prevent clogged pores. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated skin, while peppermint tea helps to open up pores and remove excess oil and dirt. Regular consumption of these teas can significantly improve acne-prone skin.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acne that are treated with herbal teas are eczema, rosacea, and dermatitis. Herbal teas such as chamomile, calendula, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation. Green tea has antioxidant properties that can help prevent damage caused by free radicals, while burdock root tea is believed to purify the blood and unclog pores, reducing symptoms of acne-like conditions.
